scope: Open-source Nixtla and FPPy guidance for nixtla_scaffold. TimeGPT is excluded.
fppy_citation: Hyndman, R.J., Athanasopoulos, G., Garza, A., Challu, C., Mergenthaler, M., & Olivares, K.G. (2025). Forecasting: Principles and Practice, the Pythonic Way. OTexts: Melbourne, Australia. Available at: OTexts.com/fpppy. Accessed on 28 April 2026.

principles.title: Simplicity is key
principles.guidance: Default workflow is load, profile, forecast, explain, export. Add advanced options only when a demo workflow needs them.
principles.source: Approved project plan; Nixtla simplicity posture

principles.title: Baselines are mandatory
principles.guidance: Always compare candidate models with Naive, SeasonalNaive, HistoricAverage, and RandomWalkWithDrift when eligible.

principles.title: Rolling-origin evaluation
principles.guidance: Use time-series cross-validation, never random splits, and compare models on the production-like horizon where history allows.
principles.implemented_by: models.py per-series cross_validation/backtest; audit/model_selection.csv; best_practice_receipts.csv id=rolling_origin_evaluation.

principles.title: Intervals need evidence
principles.guidance: Expose 80% and 95% intervals only when the model and history support them; otherwise explain why intervals were skipped.
principles.implemented_by: models.py conformal interval gate; manifest effective_levels; best_practice_receipts.csv id=intervals_supported_by_history.

principles.title: Coherent hierarchy
principles.guidance: Do not present parent and child forecasts together unless rollups reconcile under the same aggregation rule.
principles.implemented_by: hierarchy.py aggregation/coherence diagnostics now; future HierarchicalForecast reconciliation via S_df/tags.

principles.title: Nixtla source-first workflow
principles.guidance: When a modeling choice is unclear, check both Nixtlaverse docs and the implementation source. Docs explain workflow; repo code reveals supported model classes, arguments, validation helpers, and example notebooks.
principles.implemented_by: knowledge_base repo_code_map; diagnostics.md next steps; best_practice_receipts modeling hooks.

principles.title: Weighted ensembles need receipts
principles.guidance: Weighted forecasts should be learned from rolling-origin error, exposed as a named model, and accompanied by audit/model_weights.csv so an analyst or LLM can audit why each model received weight.

libraries.name: StatsForecast
libraries.apis: StatsForecast.forecast, StatsForecast.cross_validation; Naive, SeasonalNaive, HistoricAverage, RandomWalkWithDrift, AutoETS, AutoARIMA, AutoTheta, MSTL.
libraries.contract: Long DataFrame with unique_id, ds, y.
libraries.caveat: Requires correct frequency and season_length; conformal intervals need enough history.
libraries.repo_code: https://github.com/Nixtla/statsforecast/tree/main/python/statsforecast; key files: core.py, models.py, arima.py, ets.py, theta.py, mstl.py, utils.py.

libraries.name: UtilsForecast
libraries.use: Evaluation, preprocessing, losses, and plotting helpers.
libraries.apis: fill_gaps, evaluate, rmse, mase, mqloss, plot_series.
libraries.contract: Long DataFrame; evaluation expects actuals and model columns.
libraries.caveat: Utility layer, not a forecasting model library.
libraries.repo_code: https://github.com/Nixtla/utilsforecast/tree/main/utilsforecast; key files: preprocessing.py, evaluation.py, losses.py, plotting.py, processing.py, validation.py.

libraries.name: HierarchicalForecast
libraries.contract: Base forecasts plus historical data, summing matrix S_df, and tags.
libraries.caveat: Needs hierarchy metadata and base forecasts before reconciliation.
libraries.repo_code: https://github.com/Nixtla/hierarchicalforecast/tree/main/hierarchicalforecast; key files: core.py, methods.py, probabilistic_methods.py, evaluation.py, utils.py.

libraries.name: MLForecast
libraries.use: Optional v2 path for known future drivers and regressors.
libraries.apis: MLForecast.fit, predict, cross_validation; lag transforms and date features.
libraries.contract: Long DataFrame with unique_id, ds, y plus known future exogenous columns.
libraries.caveat: More moving parts; guard against leakage and only use when future drivers are known.
libraries.repo_code: https://github.com/Nixtla/mlforecast; key docs/source areas: forecast.py, lag_transforms.py, target_transforms.py, feature engineering examples, cross-validation guides.

libraries.name: NeuralForecast
libraries.apis: NeuralForecast.fit, predict; NBEATS, NHITS and related models.
libraries.contract: Long DataFrame with unique_id, ds, y.
libraries.caveat: Heavier dependencies and lower interpretability; not a core MVP default.
libraries.repo_code: https://github.com/Nixtla/neuralforecast; research-only for now, especially model modules and example notebooks.

nixtla_doc_deep_links.name: StatsForecast complete getting started
nixtla_doc_deep_links.use: Production-style workflow for multiple series: read long data, explore, train candidate models, cross-validate, and select best models.
nixtla_doc_deep_links.treasure: Explicitly names StatsForecast.plot, forecast, cross_validation and explains local forecasting/model-per-series selection.

nixtla_doc_deep_links.name: StatsForecast model catalog
nixtla_doc_deep_links.use: Model discovery for AutoARIMA, ETS, Theta, CES, benchmarks, intermittent demand, and multiple seasonality models.
nixtla_doc_deep_links.treasure: Use this before adding new model-policy branches so the scaffold chooses models based on frequency, sparsity, and history length.

nixtla_doc_deep_links.name: StatsForecast intermittent demand tutorial
nixtla_doc_deep_links.use: Sparse/zero-heavy finance series such as long-tail SKUs, low-volume products, and sporadic usage.
nixtla_doc_deep_links.treasure: Shows ADIDA, CrostonClassic, IMAPA, and TSB with plot/evaluation workflow; use before adding intermittent model policy.

nixtla_doc_deep_links.name: UtilsForecast preprocessing/evaluation quickstart
nixtla_doc_deep_links.use: Reference for fill_gaps, plot_series, evaluate, losses, and MASE-style validation helpers.
nixtla_doc_deep_links.treasure: Direct source for replacing homegrown repair/evaluation pieces with Nixtla utilities when behavior stabilizes.

nixtla_doc_deep_links.name: MLForecast end-to-end walkthrough
nixtla_doc_deep_links.use: Future driver/regressor path with lags, rolling/date features, fit/predict, cross-validation, and selection.
nixtla_doc_deep_links.treasure: Useful when moving DriverEvent from scenario overlay to true known-future exogenous variables.

nixtla_doc_deep_links.name: MLForecast cross-validation
nixtla_doc_deep_links.use: Backtesting ML regressors with lags/date/exogenous features without leakage.
nixtla_doc_deep_links.treasure: Use before promoting DriverEvent overlays into true known-future exogenous regressors.

nixtla_doc_deep_links.name: MLForecast prediction intervals
nixtla_doc_deep_links.use: Conformal intervals for ML models when future driver support is added.
nixtla_doc_deep_links.treasure: Keep interval logic model-specific; do not blindly reuse statistical intervals for ML regressors.

nixtla_doc_deep_links.name: HierarchicalForecast introduction
nixtla_doc_deep_links.use: Roadmap for coherent hierarchy: BottomUp, TopDown, MiddleOut, MinTrace, ERM, probabilistic coherent methods, and temporal reconciliation.
nixtla_doc_deep_links.treasure: Requires base forecasts, historical data, S_df, and tags; current scaffold diagnostics prepare those contracts.

repo_code_map.name: StatsForecast core
repo_code_map.use: Primary source for StatsForecast constructor behavior, forecast/cross_validation surfaces, model aliases, season_length requirements, intervals, and model class availability.

repo_code_map.name: UtilsForecast utilities
examples: docs/index examples for fill_gaps, plot_series, evaluate
repo_code_map.use: Reference for future replacement of homegrown gap repair, losses, plotting, and model-selection helpers.

repo_code_map.name: HierarchicalForecast reconciliation
repo_code_map.use: Implementation target for full reconciliation beyond current independent-node forecasts and coherence diagnostics.

repo_code_map.name: MLForecast drivers
repo_code_map.use: Future source for known-future driver models, lag/rolling feature transforms, target transformations, and cross-validation.

repo_code_map.name: NeuralForecast research-only source
repo_code_map.use: Optional future research for NHITS/NBEATS-style models when explainability and dependency weight are acceptable.

checklist.guidance: Validate unique_id, ds, y; fail fast on duplicate keys and invalid numeric targets.
Infer frequency conservatively and keep canonical season lengths by frequency; gate model eligibility separately.
When enough history exists, include rolling-origin metrics and selected-model rationale in the run artifacts.
Explain forecast vs baseline/prior year, uncertainty, data warnings, and whether driver assumptions were included.
Every run should include machine-readable diagnostics, warnings, model weights, and next debugging steps so future agents can recover quickly from failures or questionable outputs.

transformations.name: Fiscal calendar features
transformations.use: Add fiscal_year, fiscal_quarter, fiscal_month, and fiscal_period for finance review. Default GitHub-style year starts Feb 1.
transformations.caveat: Fiscal fields are explanatory features in the MVP; they do not change the forecast model unless a future MLForecast path uses them.

transformations.name: Price normalization
transformations.use: Normalize revenue by a positive factor so forecasts can separate underlying demand from pricing or definition changes.
transformations.caveat: The normalization factor must come from an auditable finance assumption or source system.

transformations.name: Driver events and scenarios
transformations.use: Apply auditable additive or multiplicative event assumptions such as launches, pricing actions, migrations, or one-time ramps.
transformations.caveat: Scenario columns are overlays on the statistical baseline, not proof the event will happen.

transformations.name: Best-practice receipts
transformations.use: Every forecast run emits a receipt table mapping FPP/Nixtla principles to runtime evidence.
transformations.caveat: A warning receipt is not a failure; it is an explicit review flag for short history, unreconciled hierarchies, or missing intervals.

transformations.name: Weighted ensemble forecasts
transformations.use: Combine eligible candidate models using inverse-error rolling-origin weights and emit the weights for audit.
transformations.caveat: Requires finite backtest metrics; otherwise the run emits a warning and the ensemble is skipped.

transformations.name: LLM diagnostics artifacts
transformations.use: Give future agents a runbook with spec, profile, warnings, selected models, weights, receipts, outputs, and next checks.
transformations.caveat: Diagnostics explain what happened; they do not replace data review or finance sign-off.

agent_recipes.name: Agent setup intake
agent_recipes.steps: Start broad forecasting requests with setup. Capture data source, number of series, interval posture, exploration mode, MCP regressor-search permission, and requested outputs before modeling.
agent_recipes.command: nixtla-scaffold setup --workspace runs\my_forecast_setup --data-source kusto --series-count many --target-name ARR --time-col Month --freq ME --horizon 6 --intervals auto --model-families statsforecast mlforecast hierarchicalforecast --exploration-mode --mcp-regressor-search --outputs all

agent_recipes.name: Excel MCP handoff
agent_recipes.steps: Read workbook or sheet, export a table, run nixtla-scaffold profile, then forecast with column mapping flags.
agent_recipes.command: nixtla-scaffold forecast --input workbook.xlsx --sheet Data --id-col Product --time-col Month --target-col Revenue --horizon 6 --output runs\excel_forecast

agent_recipes.name: DAX MCP handoff
agent_recipes.steps: Use list_connections and get_connection_context, query at forecast grain, export CSV/JSON, then run ingest so the scaffold writes canonical unique_id/ds/y plus query metadata before forecasting.
agent_recipes.command: nixtla-scaffold ingest --input dax_export.csv --source dax --query-file query.dax --id-col Product --time-col FiscalMonth --target-col ARR --output runs\dax_input.csv --forecast-output runs\dax_forecast --freq ME --horizon 6

agent_recipes.name: Kusto MCP handoff
agent_recipes.steps: Aggregate logs or finance telemetry in KQL, export CSV/JSON/columnar MCP output, then run ingest with explicit time/target/id mapping and optional immediate forecast.
agent_recipes.command: nixtla-scaffold ingest --input kusto_export.json --source kusto --query-file query.kql --id-value "Premium Overage ARR" --time-col day_dt --target-col ARR_30day_avg --output runs\premium_overage_arr_input.csv --forecast-output runs\premium_overage_arr_demo --freq ME --horizon 6
